Off Calendar Oct-23-2018 Continued To Nov-13-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Branden Meadows for Sharon Pacaldo (Personal Representative).

Mr. Meadows stated that they rejected the creditor's claim, that the date to file a lawsuit expired on September 26th, that a petition for final distribution is drafted and he needs his Client to sign it, and that he should be able to file it within the next week or so. Status on close of estate is continued to November 13, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y. S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of Estate
Off Calendar Apr-23-2018 Continued To May-21-18 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Branden Meadows for Sharon Pacaldo (Executor) via Court Call

Mr. Meadows requested a 3 month continuance, stating that the previous Executor, Gilbert Pacaldo, passed away in December, the current Executor, Sharon Pacaldo, took over in late January/February, and is in negotiation with Wells Fargo Bank in regards to their Creditor's Claim. Mr. Meadows also stated that the credit card was opened and charges were incurred when the decedent was bed-ridden and undergoing medical treatment, the Executor took it upon herself to try to negotiate down the creditor's claim rather than continue with litigation, and separate litigation is ongoing as to whether or not the suspected person who opened it is going to be held accountable. In addition, Mr. Meadows stated he believes it is a civil matter in San Francisco, and they are not Counsel in that case. The Status on Close of Estate is continued to May 21, 2018 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to file a Petition for Approval of Report on Status of Administration.

Judge: Charlene P. Kiesselbach. Clerk: Shirley Le, Rosie Noguera. Deputy: V. Jung. Not reported.

PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
Off Calendar Jan-26-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Quadra for Marcela Pacaldo. Ernest Der for Debra Dolch.

Mr. Quadra stated that there was a trial in the bifurcated matter, that counsel failed to appear, that they do not have a signed judgment yet, that the matter should proceed intestate and he will not be representing Gilbert Pacaldo. Mr. Der stated that the estate consists of a condo which is vacant and encumbered, that Gilbert should be appointed so that things can get done and that they will step aside. The Court asked how much time it would take for Gilbert to obtain counsel. Mr. Der stated that one of the pending petitions is for his appointment. The petition for probate of will and for letters testamentary and the petition for letters of administration were taken under submission to be decided upon receipt of the judgment.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Appointment For Special Letters.
Off Calendar May-13-2014 Continued To Jun-24-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: David King for William Ayoubi. Patrick Richardson for Nissim Ninio. Rebecca Coll by telephone for Marcela Pacaldo.

Mr. King stated that there is no agreement as to a neutral fiduciary, that Mr. Lucas declined to serve and that he filed a memorandum of points and authorities in support of having Mr. Ayoubi appointed. Mr. King also stated that there are insufficient assets to settle all debts, that the only asset is $250,000.00 equity in an apartment and that his Client is willing to serve without being compensated at this time. Ms. Coll stated that they had agreed to Mr. Lucas, that she spoke with Mr. Lucas and Mr. Lucas said that Mr. Ayoubi's attorney was representing him, that they have located Ms. Dolch, that she withdrew her request for the Special Administrator to oversee the West Wind business and asked that Mr. Ninio provide information to the Parties. The Court asked for filing of an ex parte petition to appoint Debra Dolch as Special Administrator. The petitions for probate were continued to June 24, 2014 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204. Ms. Coll will appear by courtcall.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy D. Jones. (Not Reported)

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Mar-26-2014 Continued To Apr-23-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: The following matters were heard: PTR-13-297152 The Angelina Pacaldo Revocable Trust and PES-13-297371 Estate of Angelina Pacaldo.

Appearances: Roger Gross for William Ayoubi. Lori Lutzer litigation Counsel for William Ayoubi. Patrick Richardson for Nissim Ninio. Rebecca Coll for Marcella Pacaldo.

Judge Cheng disclosed that he had worked with Ms. Coll several years ago. The Parties elected to proceed. Ms. Lutzer stated that there is a pending civil suit addressing the non-administration matters. Ms. Coll stated that they are aware that Mr. Ayoubi has accessed the bank accounts, that he has rented out property that is in the Decedent's estate and asked that a Special Administrator be appointed to safe guard the assets. Ms. Coll also raised the issue of operation and ownership of the West Wind Dynamic business. Mr. Gross stated that the Decedent's position is that she is the owner of the business. All Parties agreed to consolidate the Trust matter (Petition for Order Confirming Trust Assets) with the pending civil action (subject to preparation of a consolidation order). The Parties also agreed to have the probate matters remain in probate court. The Petition for Letters of Administration and the Petition for Probate of will were continued to April 23, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Court granted the request for appointment of a Special Administrator (subject to review of the order). Bond amount is to be determined.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Allen. (Not Reported)

April 10, 2014: Upon further developments, the petition to appoint a Special Administrator was not granted as to the proposed Special Administrator, Thomas Lucas, and the hearing is continued to May 13, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.
